highlights.text: For this reason I was looking higher to start this week stating on Friday at 4pm: “My lean as long as those hold we work higher to 6806, 6815, then 6851+” We hit 6863 Monday.
I wrote yesterday: “Bear case tomorrow: Short term though, the loss of 6847 could provoke a dip….

This is because there is a law in ES which I call the two siblings: These siblings are elevator down , and short squeeze: Every vertical, sharp sell in ES is followed by an equally powerful squeeze the other way.
These squeezes are caused by my core setup: The Failed Breakdown, which is the price footprint of institutional accumulation.
I wrote Thursday at 4pm: “It is much smarter to wait for the Failed Breakdown of the October 22nd 6690 low.” We got this at 1:21PM Friday and we began what ended up being a 200+ point leg higher.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into into a powerful Failed Breakdown we had below 6796 on Monday which triggered the Tues/Wed rally.

post_title: After 4 Days of Parabolic Rally, SPX Gets A Red Day. Will Bulls Buy It? November 14 Plan
